channel.title: Yahoo News – World
channel.description: Latest world news from Yahoo News

item.title: Missing Covington man found dead in France, police say
item.description: A Covington man, who had been reported missing June 22, was found dead in France, police said.
item.dc:creator: The Enquirer

item.title: Zimbabwe’s Senate approves amendment extending presidential term
item.description: Constitutional amendment will keep President Mnangagwa in office until 2030 and allow parliament to elect the president.
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera

item.title: Zimbabwe Senate approves bill to extend presidential term
item.description: Zimbabwe's Senate on Wednesday overwhelmingly approved controversial constitutional amendments that would keep President Emmerson Mnangagwa in office until 2030, giving the changes full parliamentary support after earlier consent from the lower house.Senate president Mabel Chinomona said 75 senators voted in favour and four against.
item.dc:creator: AFP

item.title: Crime boss Steven Lyons arrives in Spain after extradition
item.description: One of Scotland's leading gangland figures has arrived in Spain after losing a bid against his extradition on a series of charges related to organised crime.
item.dc:creator: BBC

item.title: Prince George Eton Decision Renews Focus on Teacher Abuse Cases
item.description: Inside the elite boarding school where royal tradition collides with criminal convictions and questions about safeguarding boys.
item.dc:creator: Complex

item.title: Israeli military: Dozens of Hezbollah fighters trapped in underground complex
item.description: The Israeli military has surrounded an underground base in Lebanon in which dozens of Hezbollah militants are trapped, Israeli officials said.
item.dc:creator: UPI

item.title: Zelensky: Russian drone signal boosters in Belarus switched off
item.description: Russian drone signal boosters in Belarus have been switched off following an ultimatum from Kiev, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky said on Wednesday. The equipment ceased operating on Monday, Zelensky was quoted as saying by Ukrainian media.
item.dc:creator: dpa

item.title: Russian opposition politician sent to penal camp over social media
item.description: Russian opposition politician Maxim Kruglov has been sentenced to seven years in a penal colony on charges of spreading false information about the military, Russian media reported on Wednesday, less than three months before parliamentary elections. Kruglov, deputy chairman of the liberal opposition party Yabloko, was convicted over two social media posts published in 2022.
item.dc:creator: dpa

item.title: US, UK, France, Germany raise alarm about Chinese patrols off eastern Taiwan
item.description: The countries said Chinese patrols off the coast of Taiwan threaten regional stability and freedom of navigation.
item.dc:creator: Defense News

item.title: Today in history, July 9
item.description: Today in history, July 9
item.dc:creator: USA TODAY

item.title: Zimbabwe's Senate approves bill to delay the presidential election and overhaul the vote
item.description: Zimbabwe ’s Senate on Wednesday approved constitutional amendments that would remove direct presidential elections, delay the next one and extend the tenure of the country’s 83-year-old leader, whose signature is the final step for them to become law. Seventy-five senators voted in favor of the bill that would postpone elections scheduled for 2028 to 2030 and extend President Emmerson Mnangagwa’s term by two years. The bill overhauls the way presidents are chosen, replacing direct popular elections with selection by lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: AP

item.title: Today in history, July 8
item.description: Today in history, July 8
item.dc:creator: USA TODAY

item.title: Hong Kong arrests two for allegedly selling 'seditious' material
item.description: Hong Kong police have arrested two people for allegedly selling items "with seditious intent", authorities said early Thursday, with local outlets reporting one of the items was a biography of jailed media mogul Jimmy Lai.The two people arrested "are suspected of displaying items with seditious intent and selling publications with seditious content inside the shop, including materials inciting hatred against the... government, the judiciary, and law enforcement agencies", police said.
item.dc:creator: AFP

item.title: Left-wing candidate concedes tight Colombia election
item.description: Colombian leftist candidate Ivan Cepeda on Wednesday conceded defeat in the tightly fought presidential election won by hard-right rival Abelardo de la Espriella.Trump offered his hearty congratulations to De la Espriella on Monday.
item.dc:creator: AFP

item.title: Will this 13-year-old girl be the next dictator of North Korea?
item.description: Kim Jong Un's mysterious heir apparent, explained.
item.dc:creator: Vox

item.title: Viral ‘dopamine sites’ simulate shopping and smoking
item.description: Experts say these apps reflect a broader trend of people seeking experiences online instead of in real life.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: US forces kill senior ISIS leader in Syria: Centcom
item.description: U.S. Central Command (Centcom), the military arm that oversees American forces in the Middle East, last week killed a senior Islamic State leader in an airstrike in northwest Syria, according to a Wednesday announcement. The June 19 strike killed Ali Husayn al-‘Ulaywi “and is part of ongoing U.S. efforts to disrupt and eliminate terrorists seeking…
item.dc:creator: The Hill

item.title: Leah Stewart Wakes From Coma After Great White Shark Mauling in Sydney (Video)
item.description: Leah Stewart emerges from an induced coma after surviving a horrific great white mauling.
item.dc:creator: SURFER Magazine

item.description: Source: Reuters
