item.description: African officials must step up financing to respond and develop vaccines for the Ebola outbreak in Congo and Uganda, the continent's top health agency said on Friday, warning that the continent cannot continue to rely on foreign partners for its health needs. The outbreak has claimed more than 200 lives out of 894 confirmed cases since May 15, with up to 35,000 contacts, the Africa Centers for Disease Control and Prevention said.

item.title: Two trains collide north of London, killing at least one person
item.description: Emergency services rushed to the scene of a collision between two trains north of London on Friday afternoon that killed at least one person. A passenger reported that he was thrown forward by the impact then saw fellow travelers with broken bones and bloody injuries. Both trains were traveling south to London St. Pancras station when they collided outside the town of Bedford around 5:15 p.m., according to information on rail tracking websites.

item.title: Ghana conference urges slave-trade nations to issue apologies and reparations
item.description: African and Caribbean leaders in Ghana on Friday urged former slave-trading nations to issue apologies and reparations over the trafficking of enslaved Africans after a landmark U.N. resolution in March declaring it “the gravest crime against humanity.” The “Next Steps” conference in the Ghanaian capital of Accra issued a declaration calling on countries involved in the Atlantic slave trade to “offer full, formal and unconditional apologies as a foundational step towards reconciliation, trust-building and reparatory justice.”

item.title: Kim Gamel, a former veteran AP correspondent in Europe and the Middle East, dies
item.description: Kim Gamel, a former veteran Associated Press correspondent who covered conflicts and uprisings around the Middle East, including the height of the Iraq war and the Arab Spring, has died, according to her brother-in-law. Gamel died Wednesday in Idaho after a yearslong battle with lymphoma, Lee Ruff said. Gamel brought the same care and compassion to her work reporting and directing coverage in war zones that she offered to her colleagues.

item.title: Mourners bury a 6-month-old Ebola victim, marking third orphanage death as Congo outbreak spreads
item.description: Mourners gathered Friday to bury a 6-month-old girl who died from Ebola earlier this week, the third child to die at an orphanage in eastern Congo as authorities have struggled to contain the latest outbreak. Carrying a cross, people stood at a distance as the small coffin was lowered into the ground by masked and gloved health workers, and a Catholic priest prayed over her body. Ituri, the region at the center of the current outbreak, has reported more than 90% of the cases.

item.title: Police probe attack on German Interior Ministry official's car
item.description: Suspected left-wing extremists have carried out a butyric acid attack in Hamburg on the private car of Christoph de Vries, Parliamentary State Secretary at the Federal Ministry of the Interior. On Thursday morning, a car owner noticed a strong, foul odour coming from his car at his home address and reported it to the police later that day, a police spokeswoman said, without mentioning de Vries’ name.

item.title: More than 70 medics infected with Ebola as DRC outbreak spreads ‘fast’
item.description: Aid cuts and poor sanitation are deepening fears that Ebola is spreading through displacement camps.

item.title: A boat with migrants capsized north off Libya, leaving 51 dead or missing, group says
item.description: A boat carrying dozens of migrant s bound for European shores capsized off the coast of Libya last week, leaving 51 people dead or missing, a monitoring group said on Friday. Ten migrants survived the shipwreck, which occurred on June 12 in the Mediterranean Sea, off the eastern part of the North African nation, according to the Abreen group, which tracks movements of migrants in eastern Libya. It was the latest tragedy in this part of the Mediterranean Sea.

item.title: Climber trapped on Everest for days says avalanche saved him
item.description: Dawa Sherpa was airlifted to Kathmandu, where doctors treated him for frostbite, severe dehydration and a fractured thigh bone.

item.title: Argentina asks US to block 13K parents from World Cup matches
item.description: The individuals are accused of failing to make child support payments.

item.title: Norway Imposes Broad Restrictions On AI For Elementary School Kids
item.description: Norway will reportedly ban young kids from using AI in schools.

item.title: Sailing the Strait of Hormuz as mines and uncertainty slow full recovery
item.description: The team's phones and satellite equipment went dark within minutes of leaving the harbor.

item.title: Mayor Accused of Faking Her Own Kidnapping
item.description: A mayor is suspected of faking her own kidnapping. Nancy Napoles is the municipal president of the Mexican town of Tenancingo and is accused of carrying out an elaborate plot to collect $2 million in embezzled public funds disguised as a ransom. Prosecutors said she was forced out of a car at gunpoint, with three people now arrested and her husband and brother-in-law on the run. No warrant had been issued for her arrest at the time of writing.

item.title: Exercise Tiger, 'Disastrous' D-Day Rehearsal in World War 2, Cost 749 Lives - Yet Many Have Never Heard of It
item.description: The movie Pressure, with Brendan Fraser starring as General Dwight D. Eisenhower, opens with a bloody scene on a beach. But it's not D-Day, although at first it looks like it. It's Exercise Tiger, sometimes known as Operation Tiger, the dress rehearsal for the Normandy invasion that turned the fate ...

item.title: Gilbert Baker’s original Pride flag takes a trip to Ireland
item.description: The first official rainbow Pride flag makes a historic journey overseas.

item.title: Toddler injured as wild boar rams into pram in Poland
item.description: A wild boar collided with a pram in the southern Polish city of Kraków, injuring a 13-month-old girl, police said. The toddler, who had been lying in the pram, had to be taken to hospital with head injuries, the PAP news agency reported on Friday, citing the police.

item.title: German journalist detained in Syria has been released, family confirms
item.description: A German journalist arrested in Syria in mid-January, Eva Maria Michelmann, has been released, her family confirmed on Friday. Her brother, Antonius Michelmann, told dpa she had been released, confirming reports published by the news magazine Der Spiegel.

item.title: Snake catcher pulls 7.5-foot black mamba from beneath bed after home search
item.description: They are fast, highly venomous, and capable of causing life-threatening bites.

item.title: Pétanque player, 68, dies after being 'hit in head with metal boule'
item.description: The man was allegedly struck by his 81-year-old opponent, who has since been arrested, the public prosecutor says.

item.title: Crewman dies after falling from cruise ship in Sound of Mull
item.description: Hebridean Island Cruises said crew responded immediately to Tuesday's incident.

item.title: German journalist detained in Syria has been released
item.description: A German journalist arrested in Syria in mid-January, Eva Maria Michelmann, has been released, her family confirmed on Friday. Her brother, Antonius Michelmann, told dpa she had been released, confirming reports published by the news magazine Der Spiegel.

item.title: Hormuz ship traffic climbs after war deal: trackers
item.description: Shipping traffic through the Strait of Hormuz rose to its busiest in two months after a deal to halt the US-Iran war, maritime trackers said on Friday.The spike came after Iran and the United States agreed this week to re-open the crucial route under an agreement to end the war, but before the postponement of talks between the sides in Switzerland that had been planned for Friday under that deal.

item.title: Funny old world: the week's offbeat news
item.description: From Germany's most surprising supporter to the duck who has become Mexico's World Cup superstar.For the 2006 World Cup -- which Germany hosted -- he started out small, commissioning a 1.5-kilometre-long flag.

item.title: Resident locks cat in chicken coop to save it from stray dogs, then finds python too full to move
item.description: The snake did not attack the chickens, apparently.

item.title: ICC's Karim Khan suspended from British bar for sexual allegations
item.description: Karim Khan, chief prosecutor for the International Criminal Court, has been suspended by Britain's Bar Standards Board for alleged sexual misconduct.

item.title: Armenia's opposition faces arrests as it contests the ruling party's election win
item.description: Several members of Armenia's opposition were arrested Friday while a major pro-Russia party asked the top court to annul this month’s election win by Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan’s ruling party, alleging electoral violations and suppression of the opposition. The opposition Strong Armenia party appealed to the Constitutional Court to invalidate election results or call a second round of voting, and denounced the arrests of opposition politicians as an assault on democracy.

item.title: ‘Wake up’: US forces Israel into Hezbollah ceasefire after stark warning
item.description: Is Donald Trump’s US-Iran peace deal already falling apart?

item.title: Iran Denies Hormuz Closure, Confirms Safe Passage
item.description: Iran Denies Hormuz Closure, Confirms Safe Passage

item.title: Suspect in child crocodile injury case out on bail
item.description: The child was in critical but stable condition, police said.

item.title: 10,000 infected in major cholera outbreak in northern Nigeria
item.description: More than 10,000 people have been infected with Cholera in a major outbreak centred on Nigeria’s conflict-hit northeastern Borno state.

item.title: Minimum age of 11 set for UK puberty blocker trial
item.description: Gender-questioning children will have to be at least 11 years old to take part in a clinical trial of puberty-blocking drugs.

item.title: Israel continues attacks on Lebanon despite agreeing to ceasefire
item.description: At least 47 people killed in Israeli attacks on Lebanon since midnight as US-Iran talks stall.

item.title: How Ukraine broke through Russia’s air defenses
item.description: As a barrage of Ukrainian drones descended on Moscow in the early hours of Thursday, Russia’s response appeared more like a scramble, and less like a well-planned strategic defense. Videos from the streets of the capital showed glimpses of the ensuing chaos.

item.title: Fatalities caused by Russian shelling in Kramatorsk
item.description: At least two people have been killed by Russian shelling in the eastern Ukrainian city of Kramatorsk. Six other people were injured, according to Vadym Filashkin, the military governor of the Donetsk region, who posted the news on Telegram on Friday.

item.title: French mountain lodges worry over strained water supply
item.description: No spring, no river, no lake: "Every litre counts" at French mountain lodges where water is scarce but visitor numbers are booming."Every litre counts, of course.

item.title: Canadian auto parts magnate Frank Stronach found guilty of sexual assault
item.description: Austrian-Canadian billionaire and automotive business founder Frank Stronach was found guilty Friday of sexual assault and indecent assault of two woman decades ago. Stronach, who is 93, had been accused of alleged incidents involving seven complainants, and pleaded not guilty to 12 charges. Ontario Superior Court Justice Anne Molloy found Stronach guilty on Friday on two charges related to two complainants.

item.title: Germany's IKEA stores affected as workers go on strike
item.description: Thousands of retail workers across Germany have once again walked out in the current round of collective bargaining. The focus of Friday's action, called by the Verdi trade union, was the furniture retailer IKEA.

item.title: Brazen attack on Niger’s airport shows jihadis are expanding to cities in Africa's Sahel
item.description: The gunfire and explosions that ripped through Niger’s main international airport are the latest sign of armed groups increasingly targeting cities and urban centers in Africa’s Sahel region, where they are competing for influence and territories, analysts say. The al-Qaeda-linked JNIM jihadi group, the most potent in the Sahel region south of the Sahara desert, claimed responsibility for Thursday's attack at Diori Hamani International Airport in Niamey that killed 11 soldiers and two civilians. It's also the headquarters of the regional alliance that bring together troops from Niger, Mali and Burkina Faso.

item.title: Hundreds of nurses given safety alarms after racist violence
item.description: The alarms were distributed to international nurses based at Altnagelvin Hospital in Londonderry.

item.title: Israel, Hezbollah agree truce, US says, as fighting derails Iran talks
item.description: Israel and the Iranian-backed Hezbollah militia in Lebanon have agreed a new ceasefire, US government sources said on Friday, after a major escalation in fighting threatened to derail diplomatic efforts to end the conflict in the region. The agreement, brokered by the United States and Qatar, came into effect at 1300 GMT, the sources said.

item.title: ‘Hideous’: Left and Right Slam Israeli Minister Declaring ‘All of Lebanon Must Burn’
item.description: Jake Tapper replied, “Ben Gvir, an anti-Arab racist zealot, says ‘all of Lebanon must burn.’ That’s almost 6 million people. Hideous.”

item.title: Photos show Nagas protesting against rising cases of sexual violence
item.description: Thousand of people from the Naga community marched in pouring rain on Friday to demand justice for victims of sexual violence after a spate of cases reported in the state. In Kohima, the capital of Nagaland, the protesters said it was time to break the silence around sexual violence for fear of societal recrimination and discrimination. The small mountain state, that has consistently ranked as one of the safest places for women in India, has been shaken by a chain of sexual assaults in the past few months.

item.title: Fisherman Missing at Sea for 7 Days Found Alive Wearing T-Shirt and Shorts in Tiny 13-Foot Boat
item.description: The man was found on Friday, June 19, in an aluminum rowboat in the northern Cook Islands, which is around 2,100 miles north of New Zealand

item.title: Ukraine is putting weapons stations on ground robots to make 'small tanks' that hunt Russia's infiltration teams
item.description: Ukraine's Frontline Robotics makes a remote weapons station that used to be stationary but can now be put on a robot to make a "small tank."

item.title: Israel and Hezbollah Agree to Cease-Fire, U.S. Official Says
item.description: The development comes after the U.S.-Iran peace talks set to take place in Switzerland on Friday were abruptly postponed.

item.title: Norway imposes near ban on AI in elementary school
item.description: Norway is imposing a near ban on the use of generative AI tools by elementary school pupils while ‌also restricting their use in the education of older children to ‌prevent a negative impact on learning, the country's prime minister said on Friday. Facing a broad ​decline in education test scores, the government in 2024 banned smartphones from schools and has given teachers back more powers to enforce discipline in the classroom. Using AI increases the risk that young children skip important steps in their education, ‌Prime Minister Jonas Gahr Stoere ⁠told a press conference on Friday.

item.title: Jordan starts international stadium construction works
item.description: Al-Hussein Bin Abdullah II International Stadium will be Jordan’s largest stadium upon completion.

item.title: Russian drones hit two foreign-flagged civilian ships in the Black Sea, Ukraine says
item.description: Local air defences shot down 79 of 90 Russian drones from Thursday evening into Friday morning, per Ukraine's air force.

item.title: Six-year-old Ebola patient taken from DR Congo hospital found and 'doing well'
item.description: Health facilities have come under attack during the current outbreak as a result of misinformation and fear.

item.title: Animal rights activists: Icelandic whaling resumes
item.description: Icelandic whalers are resuming their controversial hunt for fin whales after a two-year pause, animal welfare campaigners said on Friday. Two Icelandic whaling vessels left the port in the capital, Reykjavík, Pro Wildlife and other animal welfare organizations said.

item.description: The individuals are accused of failing to make child support payments.

item.title: At least 22 killed, 38 injured as Israel, Hezbollah defy U.S. peace deal
item.description: Fighting raged on in Lebanon with a wave of deadly of Israeli strikes killing 18 people while four Israeli troops were killed in a Hezbollah ambush.
